mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month growth in retail sales signals improving household demand and near-term revenue expectations, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the signed sales momentum is positive.
evidence_refs: fact:September 2009 vintage retail sales were available by the 2009-10-15 decision date
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than the unconditional hit rate over rolling 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment crosses into very high territory and continues rising, equity investors mark down earnings and credit quality, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 9.5% and rises by at least 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Near-zero policy rates combined with non-accelerating consumer prices lower discount rates without forcing immediate tightening, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 0.5% and 3-month CPIAUCSL growth below 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term FEDFUNDS below 0.5% with subdued CPI has nonpositive incremental predictive coefficient for 60-day returns in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production after a contraction signals inventory restocking and improving operating leverage, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change crosses above 1.0% after being negative within the prior 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:August 2009 INDPRO vintage data were available before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO rebounds have a 60-day forward return mean less than or equal to the unconditional 60-day return mean in out-of-sample evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts rising from depressed levels signal stabilization in construction, durables, and household balance sheets, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST below 700 and 3-month change crosses above 5%
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST recovery triggers below 700 are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 50% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment losses slow while unemployment remains elevated, markets price an approaching labor trough and improved forward earnings, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change becomes less negative for two consecutive months while UNRATE remains above 8%
evidence_refs: fact:September 2009 PAYEMS and UNRATE vintages were known by the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS improvement conditional on UNRATE above 8% has no positive 5-day return edge versus PAYEMS improvement without the unemployment condition.

mechanism_narrative: At the zero lower bound, an upside acceleration in consumer prices raises real income pressure and the probability of earlier tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 0.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above 3%
failure_condition: Reject if CPI acceleration triggers at FEDFUNDS below 0.5% are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A negative year-over-year real GDP growth signal indicates broad demand impairment and weak aggregate profits, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:2009 Q2 GDPC1 vintage value was the latest quarterly real GDP observation available
failure_condition: Reject if negative year-over-year GDPC1 growth is not associated with lower subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns than positive growth in walk-forward tests.
